page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core, supported by 256 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 133 Gbps. It does not include local NVMe storage or hardware GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate exceptional multi-core and memory subsystem capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier performance in memory bandwidth tests, exceeding 480 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 236 GB/sec for write operations. Multi-threaded workloads, such as software compilation with Clang, bzip3 decompression, and Passmark database operations, consistently rank in the top decile. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench text processing scoring in the average tier.

The instance exhibits a notable performance profile for large language model inference, delivering strong text generation speeds for medium and large models, but showing weaker relative performance for small model text generation. Given the high memory bandwidth, dedicated physical cores, and robust multi-threaded execution, this server is suited for memory-bound applications, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded processing tasks. The ARM64 architecture offers high resource density and power efficiency, making it a cost-effective option for scaled general-purpose workloads compared to traditional x86 architectures.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance powered by a 64-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 256 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 133 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core and memory performance, with exceptional memory bandwidth exceeding 480 GB/sec and strong multi-threaded decompression and database execution. Conversely, single-core processing and small-model LLM text generation represent relative performance tradeoffs, landing in the average and weak tiers respectively. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, offering qualitative cost efficiency through high resource density and dedicated physical cores.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 256 GB of RAM, and 133 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 133 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and dedicated multi-core processing for database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz
256 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
133 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
No local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
